statement.en: Dynamic Mitochondrial Protein Succinylation Induced by Nutritional Intervention with Omega-3 Polyunsaturated Fatty Acids Elicits Growth Inhibition and Lethality in Prostate Cancer Cells
statement.summary: Prostate cancer (PCa) continues to present a significant therapeutic challenge due to its metabolic plasticity and resistance to conventional treatments. Although omega-3 fatty acids, especially high-purity eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA), have demonstrated anticancer properties, the underlying molecular mechanisms remain inadequately understood. In this study, we provide evidence that EPA induces potent growth inhibition in PCa cells by modulating a mitochondrial succinylation network, thereby revealing three interconnected nodes of metabolic vulnerability. Through succinylomic profiling and functional validation, we identified SDHA, GOT2, and DAP3 as critical EPA-induced succinylation targets. Mechanistically, SDHA succinylation induces succinate accumulation and promotes widespread succinyl

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper describes experimental findings on EPA-induced mitochondrial protein succinylation in prostate cancer cells. The abstract is incomplete (cuts off mid-sentence), preventing full evaluation of claimed results. The mechanism appears empirically testable and the findings appear to follow standard oncology/biochemistry methodology, but the preprint status (date 2026-04-25 suggests future-dated or placeholder metadata) and incomplete abstract prevent classification as TRUE. The work is clearly underway and subject to peer review validation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 三つの標的の相互作用が、細胞死への指数的な衰退を作る。
